mission.title: argo-rollouts: Use of native SidecarContainers (init container with `restartPolicy: Always`) results in pod status getting stuck on `Init`
mission.description: Use of native SidecarContainers (init container with `restartPolicy: Always`) results in pod status getting stuck on `Init`. This issue affects 29+ users.

steps.title: Identify argo-rollouts troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your argo-rollouts deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-rollouts -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-rollouts
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-rollouts -n argo-rollouts --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review argo-rollouts configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo-rollouts configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n argo-rollouts -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-rollouts
kubectl get configmap -n argo-rollouts -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=argo-rollouts
```
K8s 1.29 enables the `SidecarContainers` feature gate by default (more info on the native sidecars here https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/sidecar-containers/)
These are defined as `initContainers` with the `restartPolicy` set to

steps.title: Apply the fix for Use of native SidecarContainers (init container with…
steps.description:
Is this fixed in 1.8.0? I see the [commit](https://github.com/argoproj/argo-rollouts/commit/6c873a95ec34e141243cc5e3846c69c9ce1c42d3) that has the fix in the 1.8.0 code, but it looks like a subsequent [commit](https://github.com/argoproj/argo-rollouts/commit/bfef7f0d2bb71b085398c35ec95c1b2aacd07187) deletes this code? I am still seeing this behavior in 1.8.0.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Confirm Use of native SidecarContainers (init container… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-rollouts -n argo-rollouts --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n argo-rollouts --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: Is this fixed in 1.8.0? I see the [commit](https://github.com/argoproj/argo-rollouts/commit/6c873a95ec34e141243cc5e3846c69c9ce1c42d3) that has the fix in the 1.8.0 code, but it looks like a subsequent [commit](https://github.com/argoproj/argo-rollouts/commit/bfef7f0d2bb71b085398c35ec95c1b2aacd07187) deletes this code? I am still seeing this behavior in 1.8.0.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo-rollouts installed or the issue environment reproducible.
